digest.component_or_area: example claude-skills test-design standard and code-review-tests reviewer
propositions: The three example skills carry duplicated inline TDD prose and lack a shared canonical test-design reference.
A shared test-design standard should be created and loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing, kept byte-identical via a drift-gating unit test.
The skills are under-specified on choosing the right test: no coverage inventory, tier-selection rule, precondition/invariant oracle guidance, fault-realism rules, or CI-gating proof.
The code-review-tests criterion should gain exactly two uncovered antipatterns (non-gating test; unrealistic fault injection / sleep-only race sync) with explicit false-positive guards.
The eval spec should add recall and no-fire cases for each new antipattern so the eval suite owns the new rubric surface.
Held-out test discipline must be preserved so the fix/implementation subagent never sees held-out test code, with plan-review E5, criteria routing, and thresholds left unchanged.
